seoTitle: Mount Seaview vs Mount Thorley — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Seaview and Mount Thorley. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Seaview vs Mount Thorley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Seaview and Mount Thorley.
heading: Mount Seaview vs Mount Thorley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Seaview, New South Wales and Mount Thorley, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Thorley has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Mount Seaview (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Seaview or Mount Thorley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Seaview has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Mount Thorley scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Seaview scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Thorley scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Seaview or Mount Thorley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Thorley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
